item.title: American Eagle rallies again after launching second Sydney Sweeney campaign
item.description: American Eagle Outfitters shares jumped on Wednesday after the denim retailer unveiled a second campaign with "Euphoria" star Sydney Sweeney, ​a year after a viral and controversial ad featuring ‌the actress fueled a stock rally.

item.title: Adobe releases AI assistant for creative tools, says it will work with Anthropic's Claude
item.description: Adobe said on Wednesday it was releasing a new artificial intelligence assistant designed to help users carry out tasks ​across its suite of software for editing photos, videos and other ‌digital content.

item.title: IMF cautions countries against broad fuel subsidies to deal with war-driven energy shock
item.description: The war in the Middle East has intensified strains on an already fragile global fiscal situation, with higher interest rates and rising energy prices already fueling calls for support from emerging markets and developing economies, the International Monetary Fund said on Wednesday in its ​Fiscal Monitor report.

item.title: Jane Street signs $6 billion AI cloud deal with CoreWeave, boosts stake
item.description: Trading firm Jane Street has committed about $6 billion for CoreWeave's cloud services, marking the third major deal for the ​Nvidia-backed neocloud company in a week and underscoring surging demand ‌for computing capacity.

item.title: Economic growth will be slower this quarter amid war, US Treasury chief says
item.description: U.S. Treasury ​Secretary Scott Bessent said on ‌Wednesday that the economy will be slower this quarter amid the ​Iran war, but it ​is in good shape and ⁠will rebound, adding that ​oil prices do not appear ​to be weighing on inflation expectations.

item.title: China's CATL beats first quarter forecasts, boosting market dominance
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle battery giant CATL posted first-quarter results that surpassed expectations on Wednesday, strengthening its market ​dominance even as EV demand softens.

item.title: Silver faces sixth year of deficit with stock drawdown raising squeeze risks, research shows
item.description: The silver market is heading for a ‌sixth year of structural deficit, with 762 million troy ounces drawn from stocks since 2021, raising the risk of a renewed liquidity squeeze despite weaker demand expectations, the Silver Institute and consultancy Metals Focus said on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump backs government AI safeguards in banking system, acknowledges risks
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump acknowledged the risks ​artificial intelligence posed to the banking system ‌and said there should be government safeguards, but also believed the technology could make the ​banking system better and safer, according ​to an interview broadcast on Wednesday.

item.title: US Treasury asks private credit firms for data, Punchbowl News reports
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department ​has begun asking ‌private credit firms to submit information ​about their ​business models and ties ⁠to the ​regulated financial system, ​Punchbowl News reported on Wednesday citing sources ​familiar with ​the matter.

item.title: Nutella-maker Ferrero aware of EU inspections, says it is cooperating
item.description: Nutella-maker Ferrero said on Wednesday ​that the company is ‌aware that on-site inspections are currently taking place ​in its offices ​by European Commission officials.

item.title: Morgan Stanley's profit beats estimates on dealmaking surge, trading boost
item.description: Morgan Stanley beat Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Wednesday, as ​the investment bank benefited from a surge in dealmaking and raked in record revenue from its ‌equities trading business.

item.title: Robots, drones could slash global food delivery costs to $1 per order, Barclays says
item.description: Autonomous food delivery robots and drones could cut costs by several dollars to as low as $1 per order, a shift ​that could unlock billions of dollars in profits for ‌the global food delivery industry, Barclays said on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump threatens to fire Powell if he doesn't leave Fed board; probe to continue
item.description: President Donald ‌Trump on Wednesday threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell from his separate seat as a member of the U.S. central bank's Board of Governors if Powell does not vacate that post ​as well when his term as Fed chief ends on May 15, intensifying a ​complicated standoff that has upended the Fed's usually smooth transition of ⁠power.

item.title: Leidos, Analogic to form security tech joint venture
item.description: Defense contractor Leidos Holdings will spin off its security and industrial automation businesses and ​combine them with security imaging and ‌detection company Analogic Corporation to form a new joint venture, the companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Recruiter Robert Walters posts smaller fee drop as hiring improves
item.description: Recruitment firm Robert Walters on Wednesday ‌posted a narrower drop in first-quarter net fees following double-digit quarterly declines for nearly three years, as its largest market Japan returned to growth.

item.title: Walmart redesigns its 'Great Value' private label brand
item.description: Walmart said on Wednesday it is redesigning its private-label brand "Great Value" ​as price-conscious shoppers increasingly opt for ‌cheaper store-brand products.

item.title: Bank of America profit rises as trading, investment banking shine
item.description: Bank of America's profit rose in the first quarter, as volatility in the global markets lifted trading activity and a rebound in mergers and acquisitions boosted the lender's ​investment banking fees.

item.title: Morning Bid: Back to business
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: PNC Financial profit jumps on FirstBank buy, strong loan growth
item.description: PNC Financial posted an 18% jump in first-quarter profit on Wednesday, buoyed by the acquisition of regional lender FirstBank and strong loan ​growth.

item.title: Lufthansa, pilots union deadlocked on arbitration offer
item.description: Germany's VC ​pilots' union and airline Lufthansa are deadlocked on ‌whether to enter arbitration after both parties put forward different conditions to take up ​the offer.

item.title: Snap to lay off about 1,000 employees after activist investor pressure
item.description: Snap said on Wednesday ​it would lay off about 1,000 ‌employees, including 16% of its full-time staff, weeks after activist investor Irenic Capital Management pushed the ​Snapchat parent to optimize its ​portfolio and improve performance.

item.title: Dollar sheds bulk of Iran war premium, but few expect a sharper drop
item.description: The U.S. dollar has given back most of the gains sparked by the Iran war, as a tentative ceasefire revived ​appetite for riskier currencies, but investors said robust demand for U.S. assets and waning prospects for U.S. interest-rate cuts should buttress it against sharper ‌declines.

item.title: US Senator Warren voices concern over Nvidia's acquisition of Slurm
item.description: U.S. Democratic ​Senator Elizabeth Warren sent a letter to the U.S. secretaries of the Department of Energy and Department of Defense ‌on Tuesday, questioning them about Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, which makes Slurm software.

item.title: Trump administration ends lease for consumer protection bureau's headquarters, records show
item.description: The U.S. Treasury's bank regulation agency has terminated the lease ​for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's Washington headquarters after 14 years and agreed to pass the premises to the federal government's ‌general real estate management agency, records obtained by Reuters show.

item.title: Fed's regional banks may be key front in battle for independence
item.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly wasn't elected by popular vote, wasn't chosen by an elected official, didn't compete against a publicly revealed slate of applicants and didn't undergo any public vetting before her bank's board of directors gave her a job that helps ​shape the U.S. economy.

item.title: Netflix to refocus on ads, content after failed Warner Bros bid
item.description: Investors will look for Netflix to emphasize content spending and ad business growth as key ​drivers when it reports quarterly earnings on Thursday, marking ‌the streaming giant's first results since its failed bid for Warner Bros Discovery.

item.title: Carmakers navigating the costly and tricky transition to battery storage systems
item.description: Converting plants to new types of batteries won’t be easy, ​nor will the demand for energy storage materialize fast enough to absorb a glut of unused factory space for EV batteries.

item.title: AI ruling prompts warnings from US lawyers: Your chats could be used against you
item.description: As people increasingly turn to artificial intelligence for advice, some U.S. lawyers are telling their clients not to treat AI chatbots like trusted confidants when their freedom or legal liability is on the line.

item.title: Volkswagen's China boss warns of fierce competition in slowing market
item.description: Volkswagen is bracing for even tougher competition in China, where the world's largest car market could ​shrink for the first time in almost a ‌decade, the head of the German carmaker's business in the region said in a newspaper interview.

item.title: UBS chairman says key business decisions may soon become unavoidable
item.description: Key business decisions may soon become unavoidable, UBS Chairman Colm Kelleher said on Wednesday, ​turning up the volume on a tense debate about ‌proposed Swiss capital requirements that has triggered growing pressure from markets and shareholders.
